codeReview.summary: The implementation adds assemble_evidence_pack() and detect_missing_links() to TraceabilityStore trait with proper new types. However, a bug in detect_missing_links() causes incorrect ResultHasNoEvidence detection: it checks whether test evidence is empty globally, rather than checking per-result evidence.

issues.description: ResultHasNoEvidence detection checks test_in_chain.evidence.is_empty() which is evidence shared across ALL results for the test, not per-result evidence. Should check if this specific result has any linked evidence. Both InMemoryTraceabilityStore (line ~1256) and SqliteTraceabilityStore (line ~2316) have the same bug.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No gaps found. Worker followed the expected conventions in the AGENTS.md and skill documentation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Cargo check, test, and clippy all pass as expected per validation contract.

summary: Implementation adds complete traceability evidence pack assembly with bidirectional links from requirements → tests → results → code → evidence. The new types (TraceabilityEvidencePack, CriteriaEvidence, TestCaseEvidence, CodeLocation, EvidenceArtifact, MissingLink, MissingLinkType) are well-structured and exported correctly from lib.rs. The feature works as described with 5 new tests covering full chain, bidirectional traversal, code locations, and missing link detection. However, a bug in detect_missing_links() causes incorrect ResultHasNoEvidence detection: it checks whether test-level evidence is empty rather than whether each specific result has evidence linked.
